content_of_post_to_answer: How do I know if I have anxiety or if I'm just stressed?
answer: A useful distinction is that stress usually points to a specific pressure, while anxiety often becomes the pressure itself. Stress tends to say “something is difficult right now”; anxiety can keep asking “what if something goes wrong?” even after the situation changes. The tricky part is that they can feel identical in your body: racing thoughts, tension, poor sleep, irritability. The better question may not be “which label do I have?” but “does this feeling help me respond to a real problem, or has it started creating problems of its own?”
